records.ety: [Pref. in- not + congruity: cf. F. incongruit.]

defs: The quality or state of being incongruous; lack of congruity; unsuitableness; inconsistency; impropriety.
quotes: The fathers make use of this acknowledgment of the incongruity of images to the Deity, from thence to prove the incongruity of the worship of them.

defs: Disagreement of parts; lack of symmetry or of harmony.
That which is incongruous; lack of congruity.
